predictions.reasoning_excerpt:
# Assessment

The National Weather Service forecast is unambiguous: today's high in NYC is expected to be **68°F**, with a peak around 12pm and hourly forecasts showing temperatures in the 65-67°F range during peak hours.

The market pricing is absurdly distorted—it has assigned 100% probability to "58°F or higher" (which is essentially a floor) while spreading 0.1% each across all other buckets. This is nonsensical given that the NWS explicitly forecasts 68°F.
